title: trifolium kentuckiense
intro: Trifolium kentuckiense, also known as Kentucky bean or Kentucky tick-trefoil, is a perennial legume native to the southeastern United States. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can also help lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Trifolium kentuckiense can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, making it a versatile herbal remedy for promoting overall health and well-being.
